notes: Exported the simulator module from src/index.ts so consumers can access mockAttempt from the package entrypoint instead of using a deep import.
Verified the public API change by running the existing targeted test script with npx tsx src/verify.test.ts after the export update.
Attempted a full typecheck with npm run typecheck; it still fails due to pre-existing NodeNext import-extension and implicit-any errors in src/cli.ts unrelated to this entrypoint export fix.
Inspected the package entrypoint and found the simulator module is already exported from src/index.ts, so no code change was needed for this focus area.
Verified the current entrypoint includes export * from "./simulator"; alongside the other core module exports.
Attempted project typecheck for validation; it currently fails due to pre-existing NodeNext import extension issues in src/cli.ts unrelated to the simulator export.
Fixed src/mutation.ts so mutateGenome derives the child strategy id from the updated generation instead of generation + 1, which previously produced ids like strategy_v3 for a generation-2 child.
Added src/mutation.test.ts covering the regression: a generation-1 parent now mutates into a generation-2 child with id strategy_v2 and matching mutation event child_strategy.

Verified with node --test src/mutation.test.ts after updating the test imports for ESM resolution. npm run typecheck is currently failing due to pre-existing repository-wide NodeNext import-extension and implicit-any errors in src/cli.ts, unrelated to this mutation fix.

Progress update: identified inconsistent error handling in src/storage.ts writeJson, which threw raw Error instead of StorageError for directory-prepare/serialize/write failures; patch and verification follow.

Added a targeted storage regression test in src/storage.test.ts for readJsonl malformed trailing partial JSONL input, asserting it surfaces as StorageError with code PARSE_FAILED, operation readJsonl, file context, line 2, and partial=true instead of a raw parse exception.
Also updated the storage test import to use an explicit .ts extension so the targeted Node test runs under ESM resolution.
Verified with `node --test src/storage.test.ts` passing. `npm run typecheck` still fails due to pre-existing unrelated src/cli.ts NodeNext import-extension and implicit-any errors.

Fixed mutationOps context_underread change tracking so the mutation event records only newly added read steps instead of the entire post-mutation read_order array, making mutation diffs precise and easier to audit/test.
Updated src/mutation.test.ts with a targeted regression test covering context_underread mutation evidence and changed-field output.
Verified by running the targeted test file with Node's test runner.

notes: Fixed verify.ts mergeFilesToProject so filesystem mkdir/write/readback failures are wrapped with actionable merge context instead of surfacing raw fs exceptions.
Added a targeted regression assertion in src/storage.test.ts covering mergeFilesToProject directory-prepare failure context alongside existing storage error checks.

Progress update: identified verify.ts mergeFilesToProject as a concrete filesystem error-handling issue; patching to wrap write/mkdir failures with actionable source/destination context and adding a targeted regression test.
Fixed src/verify.ts so captureBaseline wraps baseline file copy failures with actionable context including source path, destination path, and slot instead of surfacing raw fs exceptions.

Added a targeted regression test in src/verify.test.ts for captureBaseline copy failure handling; targeted execution is currently blocked by pre-existing ESM import-resolution issues in src/index.ts and related imports.
Verified as far as possible with `node --test src/verify.test.ts`, which fails before the new test runs due to unrelated existing ERR_MODULE_NOT_FOUND import-extension problems.

Progress update: identified src/verify.ts mergeToProject as a concrete filesystem error-handling issue and added a targeted regression test in src/storage.test.ts for source-path stat failures to ensure actionable merge context instead of raw fs exceptions. Verification is pending after fixing an intermediate test syntax issue.
Hardened src/verify.ts mergeToProject so destination-file verification failures are also wrapped with actionable merge context, instead of surfacing a raw ensureFileExists/fs exception.

Specifically, mergeToProject now catches destination existence-check failures after copy and rethrows them as `Failed to merge file <file> from <src> to <dst>: ...`, matching the surrounding filesystem error-handling style.

Verified as far as possible with `npx tsc --noEmit`, which is currently blocked by pre-existing unrelated TypeScript/ESM issues in src/cli.ts, and `node --test src/mutation.test.ts`, which is currently blocked by a pre-existing ERR_MODULE_NOT_FOUND for extensionless imports from src/mutationOps.ts.
Progress update: investigated src/verify.ts for remaining filesystem error handling in captureBaseline/createSlot/resetSlot/cleanupSlot after other agents' fixes.

Verification started with npx tsc --noEmit but remains blocked by pre-existing unrelated src/cli.ts NodeNext import/type errors; proceeding with targeted test verification for the mutation area.

Applied a targeted fix so captureBaseline now surfaces `Failed to capture baseline for slot <slot>: unable to run typecheck: ...`. Verification still pending.
